summary: Iteration 27 — Sobressalentes Edit/Delete UI buttons fix validated end-to-end. Backend: 9/9 pytest PASS (admin CRUD create+update+delete with GET verification, RBAC: tecnico GET 200 / POST PUT DELETE all 403, audit log records both 'update' and 'delete' actions on entity_type='sobressalentes' with matching entity_id). Frontend (Playwright): Admin sees add-spare-btn + edit-spare-{id} + delete-spare-{id} buttons on every card; admin successfully edits description (TEST_UI27_EDITED_{id6}) and persists in list; admin successfully deletes spare (toast 'Sobressalente excluído!' shown, card removed from DOM). Tecnico: add-spare-btn ABSENT, all edit/delete buttons ABSENT (0/0), but can list spares (10 cards visible). Fix is fully working — buttons are now always visible (no group-hover hidden class) and role check is ['admin','pcm'] both in JSX guards and backend check_write_permission.

issues: Status 'disponivel' renders as raw lowercase string (no badge styling) because it is not present in statusConfig map. Already flagged by main agent as out-of-scope for this iteration. Example: SP-275F95 'Test spare' card shows 'disponivel' instead of 'Disponível' with a colored badge.

action_items: OPTIONAL (already flagged): Add 'disponivel' (and any other valid statuses) to statusConfig map in SobressalentesPage so the badge renders styled label.

OPTIONAL: Consider showing 'Sobressalentes' link in sidebar for Tecnico role (read-only access works via direct URL but the sidebar omits it — discoverability issue). Confirm intended behaviour with product.
critical_code_review_comments: App.js L4703-4708: Edit/Delete buttons now wrapped in `['admin','pcm'].includes(user?.role)` guard with no group-hover hiding — fix is correct and matches the Add button guard at L4679.

App.js L4661: handleEdit pre-fills form with `sp.modelo || ''`, `sp.fabricante || ''` etc — fixes the React 'changing uncontrolled to controlled' warning that briefly appears when editing a spare whose fields were null at create time. Minor: also coerce `sp.descricao` to `sp.descricao || ''` to silence the console warning observed during testing ('`value` prop on input should not be null').
server.py L1929 (PUT) and L1942 (DELETE) — check_write_permission(user, ['admin','pcm']) matches the frontend guard. RBAC enforced server-side too (verified: tecnico got 403 on both write endpoints).
Audit log emission for sobressalentes update + delete is working — entity_type='sobressalentes' with matching entity_id appears in /api/admin/audit-logs.
seed_data_creation: Tests create transient TEST_SPARE_iter27_* and TEST_AUDIT_iter27 spares which are deleted in the same test. One leftover from playwright run: 'TEST_UI_Rolamento 6310' (SP-AB712A) and 'TEST_Detail Spare' (SP-F89950) and 'TEST_Rolamento 6205-2RS' (SP-CB67D7) — these are pre-existing from earlier iterations, NOT created by this run. No production data affected.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sobressalentes Edit/Delete fix is fully validated. Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_spares_rbac_audit.py (9 tests, ~4s, all PASS). Run with `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://procure-manutrix.preview.emergentagent.com pytest /app/backend/tests/test_spares_rbac_audit.py -v`. UI screenshots at /app/.screenshots/iter27_admin_ok.png (shows Novo + edit/delete pencil+trash icons + 'Sobressalente excluído!' toast) and iter27_tecnico_ok.png (no Novo, no edit/delete icons, only read view). Console emits a benign React warning during Edit modal open: '`value` prop on input should not be null' — caused by sp.descricao being null on a couple of legacy records. Cosmetic only, does not block the flow.
rca of the issue: Original bug: Edit/Delete buttons had `hidden group-hover:flex` CSS and parent card lacked the `group` class — buttons were never visible. Additionally permission gate used only 'admin' instead of ['admin','pcm']. Main agent's fix removed the hover-gated visibility (buttons now always rendered when user role is admin or pcm) and updated the role array. Validated: buttons render unconditionally on every card for admin/pcm and never for tecnico (RBAC enforced both client-side and server-side). Audit logs correctly record update/delete actions with entity_type='sobressalentes'.
